questions.question: Which of the following is an example of an out-cross in animal breeding?

options.A: Mating animals of the same breed with no common ancestor up to 4-6 generations
options.B: Mating animals within the same breed with common ancestors within 4 generations
options.C: Mating a male and a female of two different related species
options.D: Mating a superior male of one breed with a superior female of another breed

questions.question: Which product of apiculture is used extensively in cosmetics, polishes, and the pharmaceutical industry?
Which of the following hormones is not involved in superovulation in the MOET technology?
A single out-cross is usually the best recommended practice to overcome:
Select the correct combination of indigenous (Indian) cattle breeds:

options.D: Leghorn, Plymouth Rock, Rhode Island Red

questions.question: In dairy farm management, the primary goal is:

options.A: To increase the yield and improve the quality of milk
options.D: To increase the production of poultry meat

questions.question: If an animal breeder wants to combine the desirable qualities of two radically different breeds, they will utilize:
Pisciculture specifically refers to the commercial rearing of:
What is the primary reason why interspecific hybrids like Mules are generally not used for further breeding programs?

options.A: They are typically sterile due to chromosomal mismatch during meiosis
options.B: They lack resistance to common equine diseases
options.C: They undergo severe inbreeding depression immediately
options.D: Their lifespan is too short to reach sexual maturity

questions.question: The term 'Aquaculture' implies the farming of:

options.A: Both aquatic animals (fishes, crustaceans, molluscs) and aquatic plants

questions.question: In order to increase the herd size from a genetically superior female in a short amount of time, a breeder would most likely use:
Which of the following is a classic characteristic of an improved breed of poultry like the White Leghorn?
A group of animals related by descent and similar in most characters like general appearance, features, size, configuration, etc. are said to belong to the same:
During the process of inbreeding, the selective pressure primarily leads to:

options.C: The emergence of new hybrid traits

questions.question: In bee hives, the worker bees are:
Which of the following lists contains only marine fishes?
Cross-breeding gives rise to new breeds. For example, Bikaneri ewes and Marino rams were crossed to form a new sheep breed in which state?
In MOET process, a super-ovulating cow will produce approximately how many eggs per cycle instead of the normal one egg?
What is the economic significance of 'Propolis' sourced from bee hives?

options.A: It is a resinous mixture used for its medicinal and structural properties
options.B: It is the primary sugar source used in confectionery
options.C: It is used exclusively to feed the queen bee larva
options.D: It acts as a pheromone to attract drones to the hive

questions.question: The term 'Outbreeding' collectively refers to which of the following techniques?
